Imports

../core, ../data, ../strategy, ../declarative/parser, ../declarative/validator, ../declarative/strategy_builder, ../declarative/schema, ../strategies/rsi, ../strategies/macd, ../strategies/bollinger, ../strategies/crossover, ../strategies/stochastic, ../strategies/mfi, ../strategies/cci, ../strategies/kama, ../strategies/aroon, ../strategies/parabolic_sar, ../strategies/triple_ma, ../strategies/adx_trend, ../strategies/keltner, ../strategies/volume_breakout, ../strategies/dual_momentum, ../strategies/filtered_mean_reversion, alerts, schema, reports, history

Types

Screener = object
  config*: ScreenerConfig
  strategies*: seq[tuple[name: string, strategy: Strategy]]
Main screener object 
ScreenerError = object of CatchableError
Error during screener execution 
ScreenerResult = object
  alerts*: AlertCollection
  summary*: ScreenerSummary
Results from a screener run

Procs

proc applyFilters(alerts: seq[Alert]; filters: ScreenerFilters): seq[Alert] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Apply filters to alerts 
proc assessStrength(signal: Signal; indicators: Table[string, float64]): AlertStrength {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Assess the strength of a signal based on indicator values This is a simple heuristic that can be improved 
proc formatResult(screenerResult: ScreenerResult; config: ScreenerOutputConfig): string {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [TimeEffect], forbids: [].}

Generate formatted report from screener results

This is a convenience wrapper around the reports module that formats the screening results according to the output configuration.

Example:

let result = screener.run()
let report = result.formatResult(config.output)
echo report

proc generateAlert(symbol: string; strategyName: string; signal: Signal;
                   bar: OHLCV; indicators: Table[string, float64]): Alert {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Generate an alert from a signal 
proc generateSummary(alerts: seq[Alert]; totalSymbols: int; totalStrategies: int): ScreenerSummary {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Generate summary statistics from alerts 
proc newScreener(config: ScreenerConfig): Screener {....raises: [ScreenerError],
    tags: [ReadIOEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Create a new screener from configuration 
proc printResult(screenerResult: ScreenerResult; config: ScreenerOutputConfig) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Print formatted screener results to stdout 
proc run(screener: var Screener): ScreenerResult {.
    ...raises: [ScreenerError, StrategyError, KeyError], tags: [TimeEffect,
    RootEffect, ReadDirEffect, ReadEnvEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteIOEffect,
    WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}

Run the screener

This is the main entry point that:

  1. Fetches data for all symbols
  2. Runs all strategies on all symbols
  3. Generates alerts from signals
  4. Applies filters
  5. Returns results
proc scanSymbol(screener: Screener; symbol: string; data: seq[OHLCV]): seq[Alert] {.
    ...raises: [StrategyError], tags: [TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Scan a single symbol with all strategies 
proc validateConfig(config: ScreenerConfig): tuple[valid: bool,
    errors: seq[string]] {....raises: [], tags: [ReadDirEffect], forbids: [].}
Validate a screener configuration

Exports

ScreenerSummary
